commands.review_summary:
The PR removes the OpenAI/OpenAI Codex provider check from `apply_patch` tool creation in `src/agents/agent-tools.ts`.

PR surface: Source -6. Total -6 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current `createOpenClawCodingTools` requires `isOpenAIProvider` before creating `apply_patch`, so a non-OpenAI `modelProvider` omits the tool. I did not run a live non-OpenAI provider turn in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof plus maintainer/security judgment on broadening a mutating tool; automation cannot supply the contributor’s real setup proof.

target.title: fix: remove isOpenAIProvider gate from applyPatchEnabled
target.body:
## Bug

 was unconditionally gated to only work with OpenAI providers (, ) via . This prevented the tool from being available on **kimi, anthropic, qwen, stepfun, and all other providers**.

## Root cause

In :


## Fix

Remove the  check. The  format is client-side (/ markers) and is not model-specific. The remaining  check still allows per-model allowlisting via .

## Changes
- Removed  function definition (no longer needed)
- Removed  from 

## Fixes
- Fixes #88357
- Closes #45269 (related — same underlying issue of apply_patch being treated as provider-specific)

## Verification
- [x] Tool is now created for all providers when 
- [x]  still filters by  if configured
- [x] No functional change for OpenAI providers
